messages.content: Happy new year everyone!! Big year ahead. Let's crush it 🎉
Happy new year! Ready to ship. Priya here for anyone who forgot over the break lol
back. coffee in hand. let's go
omg it's already the 5th. where did January go
lin it's been 5 days
exactly. gone.
Hello everyone! First week back. Yuki here — started in December, still getting oriented 😅
Yuki!! So glad you're here. Ok quick priorities for Jan: (1) finalize pricing page, (2) get the dashboard perf issues resolved, (3) onboarding flow revamp. More in #product
hey team — already getting tickets about the onboarding flow. users confused about the API key step. flagging early
yeah we know, that's on the list. Dev can you track those in Linear so we have a count?
on it
Pricing call with the team at 2pm today. Need to nail this before we open up self-serve
confirmed. Sara can you mock up the pricing page concepts beforehand? Just rough ideas
on it. Q — are we doing 3 tiers or 4? Last I heard was 3 but nadia mentioned 4 in the all-hands
3 tiers. Starter / Growth / Enterprise. The 4th idea (Pro) was just me thinking out loud
ok great. 3 tiers it is
please also add a FAQ section to the page sara
ok recap from the pricing call: Starter = $29/mo, Growth = $79/mo, Enterprise = custom. Seat-based. Nadia — does that match your notes?
almost. Starter is $19 not $29. We want low barrier to entry
oh wait I thought we said $29 to keep margins ok
we can revisit but for now let's go with $19. I'll explain more tomorrow
wait does pricing affect the API rate limits we're coding? asking for a reason
yes — Starter gets 1000 API calls/day, Growth gets 10k, Enterprise unlimited
k. i'll hardcode those limits but flag me if they change again
they might change lol. sorry tobias
...noted
heads up — deployed new infra config last night. Should fix the memory leak we saw in Dec. Watch for any weirdness
p50 latency looking good this morning. nice
fingers crossed. Dec was rough
dashboard loading fast for me too. good sign
@dev_support how many onboarding tickets so far this week?
14 tickets in 2 days. most are about: 1) API key not visible after creation, 2) webhook setup confusing, 3) 'where do I find my workspace ID'
oof ok. Lin — can you look at the API key visibility issue this week?
yeah I can look. Tobias is the API key stored session-side or does it need a reload?
it should show on creation. let me check the endpoint
found it — there's a race condition. key is created before the UI updates. i'll push a fix today
great! let me know when it's live so I can update my canned response
API key fix is live in prod. should resolve most of those onboarding reports
testing now... yes! key shows immediately. telling users
great work tobias 🙏
np. webhook docs are still a mess though, that's a bigger lift
anyone else's IDE doing the thing where it forgets all your settings after update
which IDE
cursor
oh yeah every update. just gave up syncing settings
I use a dotfiles repo it helps a little
smart. link?
will share later, it's embarrassingly messy rn
quick update — had a long think over weekend. I want to revisit pricing. might want to go usage-based instead of seat-based
...we just coded the seat limits last week
i know i know. but I talked to 3 founders this weekend who said seat-based kills expansion revenue. hear me out at standup
do I hold off on the rate limit work then
yes hold for now
k
ok everyone just to be clear: pricing model TBD. don't ship the billing page yet sara
I literally just finished it 😭
I'm so sorry. save it we'll probably use it
standup notes: usage-based pricing is ON the table but we're not switching yet. nadia will draft a model by Friday. until then: seat-based is still the default assumption
so I can keep working on rate limits?
yes but make them configurable pls so we can swap
fine but that's an extra day
worth it
hmm. seeing elevated p99 latency on the query endpoint. ~1800ms vs baseline ~400ms. not sure if it's the deploy or traffic
just noticed too. my requests to /api/v1/analyze are slow
checking logs. db queries look fine. might be the caching layer
ok not caching. looks like the worker pool is undersized after the config change from last week
can you bump it?
doing it now
bumped workers from 4 to 8. latency coming down. p99 at ~600ms now. watching
stable at ~420ms. think we're good
nice. same issue as dec?
similar. will document so we stop doing this
TGIF! want to share — had a great call with a potential enterprise customer yesterday. They asked about SSO. Adding to roadmap
noted! SSO is already in the backlog, we estimated Q2. does this move it up?
probably not but keep it visible. also they asked about audit logs
audit logs are like 3 weeks of work
noted. not committing anything yet
does anyone actually use the dark mode on our dashboard because sara and I disagree about whether to deprecate it
I did NOT say deprecate. I said 'deprioritize fixing dark mode bugs'
same thing
absolutely is not
users definitely use dark mode. get complaints when it breaks
ok fine we keep it
thank you dev
ok sharing my pricing model draft in the doc. TL;DR: I'm recommending we keep seat-based for now BUT add a usage overage charge for API calls above the tier limit. hybrid model
ooh ok that could work. so starter = $19/seat + $0.01/call over 1k?
exactly. simple to explain, still rewards growth
makes the rate limit code easier actually. just cap soft-limit and meter overages
we'll need a usage dashboard for users to see their consumption then
yes! great catch yuki. adding to scope
lin can you scope the usage dashboard UI?
sure. how urgent?
before billing goes live so... 3 weeks?
tight but doable
yuki — can you take the overage billing logic? I'm swamped with the rate limiter refactor
sure. is there a Stripe integration already or starting from scratch
stripe is hooked up for subscriptions. you'd just need to add metered billing. lmk if you need creds
great. on it
getting a new pattern of tickets — users can't figure out how to add teammates to their workspace. about 8 today
ugh. lin — the invite flow, is it working?
it works but it's buried. you have to go Settings > Workspace > Members > Invite. no one finds it
can we add an invite button to the main nav or somewhere obvious
yeah easy. doing it now
invite button added to the user menu. deploying
live! @dev_support please test
confirmed working 👍 will update users
FYI doing a small infra maintenance window tonight 11pm-midnight PT. should be zero downtime but heads up
noted. should we post on status page?
probably. I'll do it
maintenance done. all good. upgraded postgres to 16. slight improvement on complex query times
nice. did you run the migration on staging first this time
...yes tobias. i learned from last time
just checking 😂
sprint goals this week: 1) billing page v1 from Sara, 2) usage dashboard wireframes from Lin, 3) overage billing logic from Yuki, 4) onboarding doc updates from Dev. Go team!
billing page — do we have final copy yet? I still don't know what the pricing says officially
using nadia's doc from Monday. I'll share the paragraph
please! also is it $19 or $29 for starter. I keep seeing different numbers
$19. it was changed Jan 6. sorry that got confusing
ok. $19 starter. writing it down in blood
😂 yes $19. FINAL.
rate limiter refactor done. all limits are now configurable via env. wrote docs
amazing. ready for review?
PR is up. yuki can you review? you know the billing side
on it
reviewed. looks good. left one comment about the overage calc — think there's an off-by-one on the daily reset
oh good catch. fixing
just had a 2hr call with investors. they're excited but asking about growth metrics dashboard. @priya can we scope a lightweight admin view?
define lightweight lol. like MAU, revenue, churn?
exactly that. nothing fancy. just numbers I can share on a call
we can do a quick internal dashboard. lin would this be separate from the user-facing usage dashboard?
yeah different thing. could do it in like 2 days with metabase actually
perfect. do it
anyone else notice the /health endpoint returning 200 even when db is down? that seems wrong
...that is wrong. fixing
probably explains why our uptime monitor didn't catch the dec incident
yeah definitely explains that. on it
billing page design DONE. Figma link in the product channel. super happy with how it came out
just looked — this is gorgeous Sara!! 🙌
wow this is way better than our current site. can we upgrade the whole homepage while we're at it
haha maybe after launch. let's not scope creep
fair
February! Billing goes live this month. everyone aligned?
almost. still need: usage dashboard (Lin ~done), overage logic (Yuki in progress), billing page (Sara done), payment flow testing (tobias + yuki)
target date?
Feb 16 is realistic. two weeks
ok. Feb 16 is the date. let's not slip
overage billing logic is 80% done. hit an issue with how stripe handles metered billing resets at midnight UTC vs our midnight PT. working through it
classic stripe timezone hell. you'll want to store everything in UTC and convert on display
yeah I figured as much. refactoring now. probably adds 2 days
ok so end of week for that piece?
should be
heads up — latency spike again on /api/v1/analyze. Same pattern as Jan 14
ugh. same worker pool thing?
checking... yes. workers at 8 again, not enough under current load. bumping to 12
better. p99 back to ~500ms. I need to set up autoscaling, this is the 3rd time
third time? i count Jan 14, now... what was the first
dec 18. I documented it somewhere but can't find the doc
add it to the runbook?
making a runbook right now actually
usage dashboard MVP is live on staging. please review everyone — especially @priya
checking now! ... ok a few things: 1) the 'calls today' number looks wrong (shows 0 for me), 2) the chart doesn't load on mobile, 3) love the design
calls today: is your account actually on a paid plan in staging? might be filtering
oh no. I'm on the free test account
that's why. the dashboard only shows metered plans. I'll add a message for free users
makes sense! the mobile thing though is a real bug
yep fixing
lol our error messages still say 'Something went wrong. Please contact support@example.com' in places
YES users keep emailing example.com 😭
I just grepped and found 14 instances. fixing now
bless you tobias
quick note — I updated the pricing FAQ with the hybrid model details. someone from the sales call last week asked about annual pricing discounts. nadia — do we offer annual?
yes! 20% off for annual. I can't believe we didn't put that in the doc
I don't have annual pricing on the billing page 😬
add it please! annual toggle like every other SaaS
on it. so: Starter $19/mo or $182/yr (save 20%), Growth $79/mo or $758/yr?
let me check the math... yes that's right
also: do we offer annual for Enterprise?
enterprise is custom anyway so we'll negotiate case by case
overage billing logic merged and in staging. tested against a bunch of scenarios. edge cases that still worry me: users who downgrade in the middle of a billing cycle
what happens now if they downgrade?
they keep current plan limits til end of cycle, then switch. stripe handles it ok. but overage from before downgrade — does that charge at old tier rate or new?
old tier rate I think? you used those calls at that tier
I'll ask nadia. this is a product decision not engineering
old tier rate. if you used it you pay for it
ok. makes sense. will code it that way
updating the roadmap. SSO is now Q3 not Q2. we keep getting pulled into billing work and it was always optimistic. enterprise prospect knows and is ok with it
good call. SSO is not trivial
agreed. better to do it right than rush it
onboarding issue is back. getting reports of the API key not showing again. thought tobias fixed this in Jan?
wait what. let me check the endpoint
...the fix was in the wrong service. we have two API key creation flows — one for the webapp, one for the CLI. I fixed the webapp one in Jan. CLI one still has the race condition
ahhh. so is this CLI users specifically?
yes. all the new CLI onboarding we pushed last week is hitting this
ok fixing today?
already on it. give me an hour
fixed in both places now. and I added a test so it doesn't regress again
thank you! updating users
sorry for the confusion. lesson: fix both services not just one
friday the 13th. I'm not deploying anything today
valid policy
I respect this
I will not respect this we have billing to ship
FINE. but if anything breaks...
it's on me, yes. please deploy
TODAY IS BILLING LAUNCH DAY 🚀 Let's do final checks and go live by 2pm
checklist: billing page ✅, payment flow ✅, usage dashboard ✅, overage logic ✅, stripe webhooks ✅. tobias/marcus — infra ready?
infra ready. added extra workers proactively for traffic spike. autoscaling is FINALLY set up
code is good. yuki reviewed everything
confirmed. ready from my end
billing page is pixel perfect at 1440, 1280, mobile. dev is this the nicest page on our site
honestly yes lol
usage dashboard also responding fast. chart renders on mobile now
ok we go at 2pm. someone send the launch tweet I'll approve
🟢 WE'RE LIVE! Billing is live. Self-serve is on. This is a huge milestone for the company. Proud of everyone 🙌
LETS GOOO
autoscaling already kicked in. nice to see it working
amazing team 🙌
first support ticket post-launch: 'this is awesome how do I upgrade'. I love this job sometimes
😭😭😭 best ticket ever
early numbers: 23 signups overnight, 4 upgrades to Growth, 1 Enterprise inquiry. solid!
incredible. growth plan converting better than expected
getting some confusion about the annual vs monthly toggle — users don't notice it. Maybe needs to be more prominent?
good feedback. I can make the toggle bigger and add 'save 20%' text next to annual
yes do that please sara
got a ticket: user says they saw our product on HN last month and the pricing was $29/mo and now it's $19. they're confused. asking if $29 was a mistake
we never publicly listed $29 did we? that was internal discussion
no. $19 was always the external price. they might have seen someone's tweet about our pricing who guessed wrong
ok I'll just confirm $19 is correct and has always been our price for starter
yes do that
🚨 latency spike. p99 on /analyze at 3200ms. This time it IS the autoscaling - it's scaling up but there's a 4min lag before new instances are healthy
users noticing?
error rate up 2%. yeah they're noticing
getting tickets 'the dashboard is slow'. using canned response
manually added 4 instances. 90 sec for them to warm up
ok stabilizing. p99 at 900ms. getting better
back to normal. 420ms p99. gonna reduce the autoscale lag tomorrow. need to pre-warm instances
this is the 4th time we've had this exact type of issue. we need to fix the root cause
I KNOW. scheduling dedicated time Monday. autoscaling with pre-warming
spending today on the autoscaling pre-warming. @tobias feel free to ping me if I go quiet, I'll probably be deep in docs
respect. I'll take your support tickets
I don't get support tickets
lucky you
second most common feature request lately: CSV export from the dashboard. getting like 5 requests a week now
adding to backlog. lin — how big is that?
the export itself is easy. the tricky part is what to export. all usage? by day? by endpoint? needs a spec
I'll write a mini spec. give me till Thursday
autoscale pre-warming is configured. tested it: spin-up lag went from 4min to 45sec. much better
finally 🙏
will this prevent the latency spikes we've been seeing?
should handle most cases. won't help if we get a massive sudden spike but that's what the manual override is for
quick q — does the $0.01/call overage apply to all tiers or just starter?
all tiers. same overage rate. the tiers just differ in included calls
ok. I had a comment in the code saying 'starter only' — fixing that now
I had that right in my implementation. was based on nadia's original doc
yeah I must have misread it. fixed
March sprint: 1) CSV export, 2) SSO research spike, 3) onboarding revamp v2, 4) performance optimization sprint. big month
onboarding revamp v2 — dev, what are the top issues still?
right now: 1) webhook setup docs are bad (high friction), 2) users don't understand 'workspace' vs 'project' distinction, 3) empty state on first login is confusing
the empty state thing is on me. I know it's bad. was always placeholder
I have concepts for the empty state. can I share in #design?
please yes
small thing but I noticed our JWT expiry is set to 7 days. Most apps are 24h-48h. Should we tighten this?
security-wise yes. UX-wise users will complain about logging in more often
what's the risk of keeping it at 7 days
if a token is stolen, attacker has 7 days. with refresh tokens it's less of an issue. do we have refresh tokens?
no we don't
let's implement refresh tokens and drop JWT expiry to 1h. week of work maybe
adding to security backlog. not blocking anything urgent
CSV export spec is done. posting in #product. TL;DR: export should include date, endpoint, calls, errors, latency_ms, cost_usd. date range picker, defaults to last 30 days
clean. I can implement this week
lin do you need the cost_usd calculation or should that come from the billing service?
good q. probably billing service so it matches what we charge them
ok I'll expose an endpoint for that. give me 2 days
got off a call with the enterprise prospect. they want a Q2 pilot. they specifically asked about audit logs AGAIN. @priya we need to revisit the timeline
audit logs were estimated at 3 weeks. if we start now we could have v1 by late March / early April
I can take audit logs if someone else picks up the security token work
I can do refresh tokens. I understand the auth flow
perfect. splitting it
thank you both. this enterprise deal would be meaningful ARR
does anyone know what 'synergy' means because I just got an email from a vendor saying our tools have great synergy and I've never actually known what it means
it means nothing
ok thank you that's what I thought
it means they want money
also correct
this week: csv export (lin), audit logs v1 (tobias), refresh tokens (yuki), empty state redesign (lin+sara), webhook docs (dev+tobias)
webhook docs — can I just rewrite them? the current ones were written by an intern and are... not great
PLEASE rewrite them. I've been apologizing for those docs for 3 months
yes please rewrite. dev can review for accuracy
interesting. seeing latency creep on /analyze again but this time it's NOT the worker pool. DB query time on analytics table is up. ~800ms per query
that table is getting big. do we have indexes?
we have indexes but I think we need to add one on (workspace_id, created_at). doing EXPLAIN now
confirmed. sequential scan on a 4M row table. adding composite index now
index added. query time 12ms. latency back to normal
we're going to need to think about partitioning that table eventually
agreed. not today but soon. adding to tech debt doc
users are now asking about HIPAA compliance. two separate tickets this week. nadia — is this on our radar?
not right now. we're not targeting healthcare specifically. what are these users building?
unclear. one seems to be a healthcare analytics company
tell them we don't currently offer a BAA. logging this as a signal
noted. could be a vertical to target later
CSV export is live! Users can now export usage data. I added a 'last 7 days / 30 days / 90 days / custom' picker
AMAZING. the feature request people are going to love this
great work lin! did you include the cost column?
yes. pulls from yuki's billing endpoint
👍 tested it too. amounts match our invoices
audit logs v1 is done. only covers API calls for now. UI events will be v2
does it have enough for the enterprise prospect?
depends what they need. it logs: who called what API, when, from which IP, what response code. is that enough?
I'll check with them Monday. if it is we can demo it next week
enterprise prospect says audit logs are 'good enough for the pilot'. scheduling demo for Wednesday. @tobias can you prep a sandbox?
yes. will have it ready Tuesday EOD
who's joining the demo call?
me and tobias. maybe sara for design questions
happy to join
ok I know I keep doing this but — enterprise prospect asked if we can do a per-workspace model instead of per-seat. basically flat fee per workspace
that's... completely different from what we built
only for enterprise. starter and growth stay seat-based
enterprise is custom pricing anyway so technically we can charge however we want
from billing perspective: if enterprise is custom invoice, I can just create a flat-rate subscription in stripe manually. no code changes needed
perfect. so no engineering work, just a sales decision
ok. just please document whatever we agree on so we stop being confused
I'll update the pricing doc today. final answer
demo call in 1hr. nervous-excited
sandbox is loaded. test data looks realistic. you're good
good luck!! you've got this
THAT WENT REALLY WELL. They love the product. They want to run a 30-day pilot starting April 1. Details TBD but this is huge
nice work everyone who contributed to the features they saw
😭😭 so proud
can I start telling users we have an enterprise tier?
not yet. wait til it's signed. but soon!
refresh token implementation is done and tested. JWTs now expire in 1 hour, refresh tokens valid 30 days. refresh happens silently in the background
nice. any regressions in auth?
nope. tested full login/logout/refresh cycle. also tested concurrent requests during refresh — no issues
beautiful. merging
empty state redesign is live. Sara's illustration looks amazing. also added a 3-step quick start guide in the empty state
🥹 thanks lin! it was fun to design something a bit whimsical
just tested it. this is SO much better than the old 'No data yet.' message with a grey box
this should help onboarding a lot. great work you two
this week: prep for enterprise pilot April 1. need: onboarding guide for them, their workspace set up, point of contact for support, SLA commitment?
no formal SLA for pilot. but I told them we'd respond to issues within 2 hours during business hours
I can be primary contact for the pilot customer. I'll set up a shared Slack channel with them
perfect. that's what I was going to suggest
I'll set up their workspace with elevated API limits for the pilot. what limits should they get?
unlimited for the pilot. remove all caps on their account
done. workspace ID is in the ops doc
writing the enterprise onboarding guide. question: should I mention audit logs given they're v1 and limited?
yes but be honest about what's included. 'API call audit logs with IP tracking. UI event logging coming in Q2'
also mention that log retention is currently 90 days. we haven't built the custom retention setting yet
good to know. adding that caveat
just be upfront. they're pilot customers, they know it's not fully baked
another latency event. p99 at 2100ms. this time it's new. the analytics table has grown to 9M rows and the index I added on March 10 is getting bloated
we talked about partitioning. is now the time?
I'm going to do range partitioning by month. it'll require a maintenance window. can we do Sunday night?
yes. post to status page as scheduled maintenance
in the meantime I'm using a partial index (last 30 days) as a bandaid. latency back to 650ms
acceptable for now. let's do the partition Sunday
just noticed: the pricing page still says '1000 API calls/day' for starter but in the last team update nadia said it's 1000/day OR 30,000/month, whichever comes first. which is it?
ugh. it's 1000/day. the 30k/month was an idea that was NOT approved. who said that in the update?
notes from the Jan 19 standup. I have it written down as '30k/month'
let me look... oh I see it. nadia said it as an OPTION not a decision. the decision was 1000/day
correct. 1000/day. sorry for the confusion. I think I floated 30k as an alternative framing and it wasn't clear it was rejected
ok. keeping the page as is then. and I'm documenting this in the pricing bible doc
great idea. we need a single source of truth
reminding everyone: DB maintenance Sunday 11pm-1am PT. analytics writes will be paused, reads will work. posting to status page now
any risk of data loss?
no. we'll drain the write queue before starting. worst case is a few minutes of buffered writes being delayed
sounds good. I'll be around on-call just in case
appreciate it. hopefully boring
does anyone work Saturdays? asking for a friend (it's me)
unfortunately yes. but only because I procrastinated all week
same. catching up on review comments
ok good I don't feel as bad
starting maintenance window. draining write queue now
migration started. partitioning analytics table by month
migration done. validating data integrity
all checks pass. re-enabling writes. 12:30am, 30min ahead of schedule 💪
nice work marcus. get some sleep
on it. query times looking amazing btw. 8ms on the analytics table now
last week of March. enterprise pilot is 2 days away. how's everyone feeling?
nervous but ready. their onboarding guide is done. shared Slack channel is set up
their workspace is configured. audit logs are enabled. API limits are uncapped
infra is in the best shape it's ever been after last night. perfect timing
great. we're ready. excited for this
getting renewed complaints about onboarding again. specifically the workspace vs project confusion. even after lin's empty state fix, users still misunderstand the hierarchy
this is the third time this has come up. we need to fix the mental model, not just the UI
it's a naming problem honestly. 'workspace' and 'project' are used interchangeably in some places
I know three places in the app that call the same thing different names. it's a mess
ok I'm declaring this an April priority. we need a terminology audit. lin + sara own this. dev you document the user confusion cases
on it
April 1st and enterprise pilot officially begins today! Not an April Fools joke 🙏
just messaged them in our shared channel. they're onboarding their first users now
monitoring their API usage. looks like they're making test calls. system is happy
this is so exciting. let's give them space to explore but be available
enterprise customer feedback from day 2: they LOVE the audit logs. They said it's 'exactly what their compliance team needed'. Also asked if we can export audit logs to CSV
audit log export. that's like 2 hours of work
adding to sprint. let's get it to them this week as a nice surprise
on it
oh no. API key not showing on creation — AGAIN. Getting multiple reports. Is this the same bug?
SERIOUSLY. checking now
found it. we have a THIRD API key creation flow — the integrations page. I only knew about webapp and CLI. this one also has the race condition
how many flows are there for creating API keys??
apparently at least three. fixing now AND doing a full codebase audit
I'll help with the audit. this is embarrassing
ok audit done. found 3 flows total, all now fixed. also wrote a shared utility function so there's only ONE implementation of API key creation going forward
can we please add this to the automated test suite
already did. 4 tests covering all 3 flows plus the shared function
updating users. this is the 3rd time I've had to do this. let's hope 4th time isn't a thing
it won't be. I promise.
sprint goals: terminology audit (lin+sara), audit log CSV export (tobias - almost done?), JWT review, onboarding docs v3
audit log CSV is done actually. shipped Friday. already told the enterprise customer
they were SO happy about that. responded within 10 minutes
that's the kind of responsiveness that wins enterprise contracts 🙏

updating everyone: pricing is OFFICIALLY finalized and locked. Starter $19/mo ($182/yr), Growth $79/mo ($758/yr), Enterprise custom. All tiers: $0.01/call overage above daily limit. Starter: 1000 calls/day, Growth: 10k calls/day. This is NOT changing. It's in the pricing bible doc. Go look.
PINNING THIS MESSAGE
thank you. bookmarked.
I already have this on the pricing page. finally the source of truth matches reality
did a spike on SSO. SAML vs OIDC. My recommendation: OIDC with WorkOS as the provider. Handles all the enterprise IdP stuff, 3-4 week integration instead of 12+
cost of WorkOS?
it's per-connection. first connection is free, after that ~$125/mo per connection
if enterprise is $2k+/mo we can absorb that. approved. let's use WorkOS
great. starting Q2. probably May

terminology audit done. Found: 'workspace' and 'organization' used interchangeably in 34 places. 'project' and 'workspace' confused in 12 places. Proposing: ORGANIZATION at the top level (company), WORKSPACE below that (team or department), PROJECT is deprecated — everything is a workspace
I agree with this. 'Project' caused most of the confusion. Users thought 'project' meant a specific task, not a container
this is a meaningful rename. does it require a DB migration?
yes but it's just renaming columns. I'd say 1 week to do safely with zero downtime
let's do it. users are worth the effort. can we also update the docs?
I'll update all the docs and canned responses once the rename is live
big week: terminology rename, enterprise pilot week 2, onboarding v3 launch
starting the rename migration today. going very carefully
enterprise customer week 2 check-in call at 3pm with nadia. prepped questions
terminology rename is 60% done. renamed all DB columns, updating API responses now. backwards compatibility shim in place so old API clients don't break
what does the backwards compat shim do exactly?
API responses include both 'workspace' and 'project' keys for now, both pointing to same data. Old clients get 'project', new clients get 'workspace'. Will deprecate 'project' in 90 days
smart. does that affect billing?
billing uses workspace_id internally which is stable. you're fine
enterprise pilot week 2 check-in: they're very happy. using it daily. their main ask: more granular role permissions (read-only viewer role)
viewer role — how big is that?
depends how granular. basic viewer (can't call APIs, can only view dashboard) is 1 week. RBAC with custom permissions is months
start with basic viewer. that's what they actually need
can do. Q2 scope

onboarding v3 is live! Changes: new empty state (already had this), terminology updated (organization/workspace), step-by-step quickstart guide, tooltip hints on first visit, webhook setup is now a wizard instead of docs-only
the webhook wizard is going to save me SO many support tickets. testing now
wizard is clear! went through it with zero issues. the docs were the #1 friction point for months
great work lin, tobias, sara. this has been a long time coming
it's so much better. proud of this one
quick note: latency has been rock solid since the DB partition. p99 stable at 380ms for 3 weeks. autoscaling working well. tech debt is paying off
appreciate the update. feels weird to not be firefighting
enjoy it while it lasts
cursed
last 10 days of April. pushing to close the month strong: enterprise contract (nadia), viewer role (tobias), SSO kickoff, Q2 planning
enterprise contract: they've verbally committed. legal review started. hoping to sign by April 30
starting viewer role today. want to have it ready when they sign

running through open feature requests. top items by volume: 1) CSV export ✅, 2) viewer role (in progress), 3) Slack notifications for usage alerts, 4) bulk API key management, 5) custom webhook retry logic
Slack notifications is actually pretty easy to build right?
yeah. Slack incoming webhooks. day or two. who wants it?
I can do it after viewer role lands. or in parallel
parallel is fine. put it in the sprint
we've been a company for how long and there's still no team photo on the about page
I've asked about this 4 times. nadia keeps saying 'when we're bigger'
ok FINE we can do a virtual team photo. lin can you coordinate?
absolutely not. I can barely coordinate my own wardrobe
I'll do it. can everyone send me a headshot by Friday?
my LinkedIn photo is from 2019 is that ok
absolutely not Dev please take a new one
got a question from a potential customer: 'is the $0.01/call overage charged in real-time or at end of month?'
end of billing cycle. we sum up all overages and charge once via Stripe. real-time would require a different billing architecture
great thanks. do we document this anywhere?
it's in the FAQ on the billing page. might not be prominent enough. sara — can you check?
found it. it's the 5th FAQ item. I can move it to 2nd position since it's clearly a common question
yes please

viewer role is shipped. users with viewer access can see all dashboards and usage data but cannot: create/delete API keys, modify settings, invite members, or make API calls via the UI. tested with enterprise sandbox
perfect timing. sharing this with the enterprise team today
enterprise customer is going to love this. they asked for it specifically for their compliance team members
final week of April! enterprise signing, Slack notifications (yuki), Q2 planning session Thursday
Slack notifications are live. users can configure alerts for: 80% usage threshold, 100% usage (overage starts), unusual API error rates. all configurable per workspace
amazing. this will stop the 'I didn't know I went over my limit' complaints
great work yuki! any docs needed?
wrote a setup guide. it's live in the docs site
enterprise contract is SIGNED! 12 month deal. Annual. This is our first enterprise customer! 🎉🎉🎉
YESSSS!!! 🚀🚀🚀
LETS GOOOOO
incredible. wow
we actually did it 🎉
so amazing. congrats everyone!!
I'm literally tearing up. we did this together
couldn't have done it without every single person in this channel. the audit logs, the viewer role, the reliability work, the onboarding — it all contributed. proud of this team more than I can say
Q2 planning session recap: priorities are SSO (May), RBAC v2 (May/June), API versioning (June), multi-region (July stretch). Plus: keeping the enterprise customer very happy
SSO starting next week with WorkOS. excited to use it. heard good things
for multi-region — that's probably more of a Q3/Q4 thing given the complexity. let's not overcommit
agreed. marking it as Q3. Q2 is already full
from infra side: multi-region requires significant work. glad to scope it properly in Q3. want to do it right

end of April. reflecting: in 4 months we went from a pricing spreadsheet and a broken API key flow to a signed enterprise contract. it's been chaotic and hard but this team is incredible. Q2 is going to be even bigger. 🙏
what a ride. also: onboarding is actually pretty good now, which I didn't think would happen this quarter
ticket volume is down 40% from January. partially because the product is better, partially because the docs are better. both things
and the API key bug is fixed in all three places this time. with tests. I checked again just now
lmao tobias has PTSD from that bug
you don't understand what it was like. three times dev
I understand exactly what it was like
see everyone in Q2. infra is stable. I'm taking Saturday off for the first time in months
you've EARNED it. see you Monday everyone 💙
